data.body: PASS 1 review (T1 Prior-art / novel-architecture justification — overlay): PASS. The plan crosses a bright-line (a novel-ish "producer↔consumer snapshot contract test through the production path" pattern), but it affirmatively satisfies T1 rather than reinventing in a vacuum: (a) prior art is explicitly considered — the guardrail/ADR note is required to record "why one producer↔consumer contract test through the production path, rather than Pact or a live sandbox," naming the two established alternatives and justifying the choice; (b) the validation posture cites known prior art ("Dredd/Gavel posture": assert structure/type, not exact values), so the contract-test approach is anchored to an established practice, not repackaged novelty; (c) no unverified capability assertions — TypedDict + JSON Schema is a well-trodden, standard pattern, and the fetcher→differs→applier production seam already exists in-tree (fetcher.fetch_snapshot, inbound/outbound differs, applier). No grounded T1 finding.
